div1.entryFree:
Passionate, adj. 1) expressing great commotion of mind: Mids. III, 2, 220. H6B I, 1, 104. Hml. II, 2, 452.
2) sorrowful: poor forlorn Proteus, p. Proteus, Gent. I, 2, 124. warble, child; make p. my sense of hearing, LLL III, 1 (Armado's speech; cf. Merch. V, 69). she is sad and p. John II, 544.
3) Misapplied for compassionate: this p. humour of mine, R3 I, 4, 121 (Qq my holy humour).
